creator: tamelingdaniel@gmail.com
text:
I think that the kevent in forward.c doesn't return for the truncation itself but only after the truncation + write. At that point, the code can't figure out whether it needs to reread the entire file. It just sees the difference in file sizes. See for example:

$ touch test.txt                                                                                                                                                          
$ echo -n 123 > test.txt                                                                                                                                                 
$ echo -n 456789 > test.txt                                                                                                                                               
$ echo -n a > test.txt                                                                                                                                                    
$ echo -n bcdef > test.txt

gives the output

$ tail -f test.txt                                                                                                                                                        
123789cdef
